hints.animus: 'animus' is a noun — the subject of the sentence

hints.ferio, ferire, ferivi, feritum: These would be the parts of 'ferire' (to strike) — 'ferre' is highly irregular
hints.sum, esse, fui, futurum: These are the principal parts of 'esse' (to be)
hints.facio, facere, feci, factum: These are the principal parts of 'facere' (to make)

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'animus' with -us ending is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied nominatives — 'animus' is a regular 2nd declension -us noun
hints.4th: 4th declension -us nouns have genitive -us — 'animi' genitive is 2nd declension

hints.Present active participle: Present active participle would be 'mutantes' — 'mutatas' has a perfect passive -t- stem
hints.Gerundive: Gerundive would be 'mutandas' — 'mutatas' is the 4th principal part stem
hints.Supine: The supine would be 'mutatum' — 'mutatas' is accusative plural feminine participle

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural of 'forma' would be 'formae' — 'formas' with -as is accusative

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'forma' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'forma' is a typical 1st declension -a noun
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'forma' is 1st declension

hints.Ablative: 'in' + ablative means 'in/within' (location) — here 'in nova' means 'into new (forms)', implying motion/change

translation: My spirit leads me to tell of forms changed into new
